Source: [here](https://sadashree.substack.com/p/why-savarkar-never-got-his-due).

Why Savarkar never got his due?

# Why Savarkar never got his due?

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 26, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

Had answered the question [here](https://www.quora.com/Why-did-Indian-history-always-neglect-Veer-Savarkar/answer/Ratnakar-Sadasyula-%E0%B0%B0%E0%B0%A4%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%A8%E0%B0%BE%E0%B0%95%E0%B0%B0%E0%B1%8D-%E0%B0%B8%E0%B0%A6%E0%B0%B8%E0%B1%8D%E0%B0%AF%E0%B1%81%E0%B0%B2?__filter__=5&__nsrc__=1&__sncid__=12604003001&__snid3__=17978168835) at Quora, resharing.

One of the reasons was his alleged involvement in the Mahatma Gandhi assassination. Savarkar had long been a bitter critic of Gandhi, calling him a sissy and a hypocrite, and condemning his support to the Khilafat movement. Nathuram Godse, was a member of the Hindu Mahasabha and RSS, to which Savarkar was also associated with. Savarkar had invested around 15,000 Rs in Godse’s company “Hindu Rashtra Prakashan Ltd”, that published the Agrani, a Marathi daily. Being a former president of the Hindu Mahasabha of which Godse was a part, Savarkar was arrested post Gandhi’s murder and kept in prison. This in spite of the fact that Savarkar had condemned Gandhi’s assassination, saying it would endanger India in it’s nascent stage.

[](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!A-NJ!,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3210febf-2eb3-48f7-9aeb-d97decf9b150_300x281.webp)

![](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!A-NJ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fbucketeer-e05bbc84-baa3-437e-9518-adb32be77984.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F3210febf-2eb3-48f7-9aeb-d97decf9b150_300x281.webp)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Even though Godse, claimed full responsibility for the assassination, the approver Badge, claimed that Narayan Apte and Godse, met Savarkar before committing the act. As per Badge, Apte is believed to have said, that Savarkar blessed them and claimed Gandhi’s years were over. However the court struck down the case, and Savarkar was acquitted. That taint however refused to go away, he was seen as a party to Gandhiji’s assassination. His home in Dadar was attacked by angry mobs. Even after being acquitted of charges, he was arrested by the Government for what they alleged as “militant Hindu” speeches. Under Nehru’s rule, Savarkar became persona non grata, Congress leaders were forbidden to participate in any functions honoring him. Nehru rejected outright, the proposal by Sardar Patel and CD Deshmukh post Independence to have any alliance with the Hindu Mahasabha. Nehru in fact wanted Cellular Jail to be demolished and a hospital built in it’s place. The Home Minister, Y B Chavan did not visit Savarkar’s prison in Cellular Jail, nor did Morarji Desai when he went to Andamans as Prime Minister.

Other reason, was his mercy petitions to the British, during his tenure in Cellular Jail. Most modern day historians, have used this to play him up as a British stooge and coward, who grovelled in front of them. Again, to call some one who spent 12 years of the most degrading captivity in a place that was equivalent to Auschwitz or the Soviet Gulags, would be doing a great disservice to some one who gave up his all for India’s freedom. Even his mercy petition was more a tactical ploy, of taking a step back, for the time being. The fact is even after his release from Cellular Jail, he was kept under house arrest at Ratnagiri, and every move of his was monitored. So much for being a British stooge.
